pattern: BFS from every node in a small graph (n <= 100) built from the linear chain plus one extra edge gives exact shortest distances; accumulate pair counts per distance.
hints: Build an adjacency list for the n-node graph: add edges i<->i+1 for all i, plus the shortcut x<->y (if x != y).
Run BFS from each house to find shortest distances to all others; increment result[dist-1] for each reachable house at that distance.
The answer array is 1-indexed with length n, and counts ordered pairs (both directions), so each unordered pair contributes 2.

edge_inputs.description: x equals y, extra edge is a self-loop (no effect)
shortcut connects endpoints, forms a cycle
shortcut creates alternate shorter path not obvious by hand

stress.description: random n, x, y within constraints
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, 100)
    x = rng.randint(1, n)
    y = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [n, x, y]
